delegatedBy.description: Track incomplete tasks + stress test P49-P57

turns.content:
You are hm-phase-researcher. Track incomplete tasks across the project and map them to recent phases (P49-P57) for stress testing.

## Output target
.hivemind/planning/incompletion-and-stress-test-2026-06-04.md

## Scope

### A. Incomplete task inventory
Search for incomplete tasks:
- .planning/todos/pending/ (or equivalent) — list all pending todos
- .planning/phases/49-*/SUMMARY.md (if exists) — look for "FOLLOW-UP" or "TODO" sections
- .planning/phases/50-*/SUMMARY.md — same
- .planning/phases/51-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/52-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/53-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/54-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/55-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/56-*/SUMMARY.md
- .planning/phases/58-*/SUMMARY.md — list all follow-ups from P58 extension

Also check:
- .planning/ROADMAP.md — phases with status != "Complete"
- Grep for "FOLLOW-UP", "TODO", "DEFERRED", "BACKLOG" across .planning/
- .planning/USER-PAIN-BACKLOG.md (if exists) — list S1-S4 statuses

### B. Map incomplete tasks to recent phases
For each incomplete task, identify:
- Originating phase (P49-P58)
- Why it's incomplete (deferred, blocked, scope-cut, lost in noise)
- Which recent phase (P49-P57) would catch it
- The real-life use case that exposes it

### C. Stress test plan for P49-P57 in real OpenCode runtime
For each phase P49-P57, identify the 1-2 most stress-test-worthy scenarios:

**P49 tmux-e2e-completion:** tmux session lifecycle, abort/resume, completion detection under load
**P50 cleanup opencode-tmux fork:** verify no orphan processes after harness restart
**P51 synthesize core tmux classes:** verify TmuxMultiplexer is the single source of truth (not fork)
**P52 wire tmux-copilot + state query:** verify all 7 tmux-copilot actions work in real OpenCode
**P53 live pane monitoring:** verify pane-captured events fire on real activity
**P54 session persistence + restart-recovery:** kill harness, restart, verify sessions resume
**P55 E2E UAT:** verify the 4 seed criteria still hold in real runtime
**P56 stress test:** verify performance under load (10+ concurrent delegations)
**P58 gap-fix:** verify S1-S4 in real runtime (covered separately by user)

For each scenario:
- Setup: what the user needs to do
- Expected: what should happen
- Failure indicator: what would indicate the phase didn't hold
- Verify command: BATS or vitest or manual

### D. Real-life use cases (to make tasks "real")
List 5-10 concrete real-life workflows the user can do in OpenCode:
- "Dispatch 3 parallel research tasks, monitor all 3, abort the slow one, inject into the fastest"
- "Run a long-running code review task, while it runs dispatch a quick delegation to fix a typo in a different file"
- "Open 5 tmux panes (one per delegation), switch between them, verify each streams correctly"
- "Force a harness restart mid-delegation, verify the delegation resumes or fails gracefully"
- "Run 10 sequential BATS test invocations, verify no state leaks between"
- "Read a 500-line file via delegate-task, verify peek/progress update in real time"

### E. Risk-prioritized stress test sequence
Order the 5-10 real-life use cases by:
- Likelihood of exposing latent issues
- Coverage of recent phases (P49-P57)
- Time required (start with 5-min tests, end with 30-min tests)

## Output format

```
# Incompletion Inventory + P49-P57 Stress Test — 2026-06-04

## Section A: Incomplete Task Inventory
| Task | Originating Phase | Status | Why Incomplete | Last Touched |
List with one row per incomplete task.

## Section B: Task-to-Phase Mapping
| Task | Maps To Phase | Real-Life Use Case |
| One row per task.

## Section C: P49-P57 Stress Test Scenarios
For each phase:
### P[N] [name]
- Stress test 1: [name]
  - Setup
  - Expected
  - Failure indicator
  - Verify command

## Section D: Real-Life Use Cases (10)
For each:
- Use case N: [title]
- Steps (concrete commands)
- Phase coverage (which P49-P57 phases it exercises)
- Time required
- Verdict criteria

## Section E: Recommended Stress Test Sequence
Ordered list of use cases to run, with rationale for ordering.

## Summary
- Total incomplete tasks: count
- Total stress test scenarios: count
- Total real-life use cases: count
- Estimated total time to run all: X minutes
- Recommended starting point: use case N
```

## Constraints
- L5 documentation only
- DO NOT modify any source code, tests, or .opencode/
- ONLY write to .hivemind/planning/incompletion-and-stress-test-2026-06-04.md
- Mark as L5
- Use grep/glob, do not full-read large files
- Target 250-500 lines

## Return
- File path
- New line count
- Total incomplete tasks found
- Total stress test scenarios
- Total real-life use cases
- Recommended starting use case (1-line reason)
I'll perform this research task systematically. Let me start by surveying the project structure and gathering data in parallel.
Track incomplete tasks + stress test P49-P57
